subsections.title: 1.1 Draw and explain the food pyramid guide in its various forms.

content.value:
The **USDA Food Pyramid** (traditional) and **MyPlate** are both widely used.

**Key principles (Healthy Eating Food Pyramid):**

items: **Eat Moderately** – Meat, fish, eggs, beans, and milk/dairy alternatives

content.value: *What is a serving?* A serving is a measured amount used to quantify daily intake; it’s different from a portion (the amount you actually eat).

subsections.title: 1.2 Why is it important to eat a balanced diet?

items: **Reduces chronic disease risk** – heart disease, diabetes, some cancers, high blood pressure.
**Improves mental health** – may reduce depression and anxiety.
**Promotes growth & development** – vital for children and teens.
**Improves skin, hair, appearance** – nutrients keep tissues healthy.
**Supports long‑term health** – lays foundation for a healthy life.

sections.title: 3. Two‑Day Lacto‑Ovo Vegetarian Menu (Balanced, following the food guide pyramid)

rows: Whole‑grain oatmeal with berries and low‑fat milk; glass of water
Lentil soup; whole‑wheat roll; mixed green salad with vinaigrette; apple
Vegetable stir‑fry (broccoli, carrots, bell peppers) with tofu; brown rice; orange slices
Scrambled eggs with spinach; whole‑wheat toast; sliced banana; water
Chickpea salad sandwich on whole‑grain bread; carrot sticks; pear
Baked sweet potato; steamed kale; black beans; side of melon

content.value: *Ensure fluids (water, herbal tea) throughout the day.*

rows: Oranges, red bell peppers, broccoli, strawberries
Whole grains (brown rice), legumes (beans, lentils), sunflower seeds
Red meat, spinach, lentils, beans, iron‑fortified cereals
Dairy products (milk, yogurt, cheese), sardines (with bones), fortified plant milks, kale

sections.title: 6. Why Drink Plenty of Water Every Day?

items: Flushes out waste products (urination, perspiration, bowel movements).
Transports nutrients and oxygen to cells.
Prevents dehydration (which causes headaches, fatigue, dizziness).
Reduces risk of kidney stones and UTIs.

sections.title: 7. How Much Water to Drink Daily

content.value:
**Men**: about 14.8 glasses (3.7 liters)

**Women**: about 10.8 glasses (2.7 liters)

*(Source: Mayo Clinic)*

sections.title: 8. Three Common Diseases Controlled by Diet

rows: Reduce saturated fats, sugar, processed foods; increase fruits, vegetables, whole grains.
Reduce salt, alcohol, saturated fat; increase vegetables, fruits, whole grains.
Balance carbohydrate intake (low‑glycemic); high‑fiber foods improve insulin sensitivity; reduce sugar.
Eat iron‑rich foods (red meat, spinach, beans) with vitamin C to enhance absorption; supplement if needed (consult doctor).

sections.title: 9. Whole Wheat Flour vs. White Flour

items: **Whole wheat flour** retains the bran, germ, and endosperm → more fiber, vitamins, minerals.
**White flour** is refined – bran and germ removed → finer texture but lower fiber and nutrients.

content.value:
**RDA = Recommended Dietary Allowance**

The average daily intake level of a nutrient sufficient to meet the requirements of nearly all (97–98%) healthy individuals in a specific life stage and gender group. It is used as a goal for daily intake.
